title: ActiveMQ Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) Accumulation
dataSources: `index=messaging` `sourcetype=activemq:broker` destination metrics for `ActiveMQ.DLQ` or custom `*.DLQ` queues (`QueueSize`, `DestinationName`, `EnqueueCount`)
app: Splunk Add-on for JMX (Splunkbase 2647) with ActiveMQ MBean templates emitting `activemq:broker`
spl:
index=messaging sourcetype="activemq:broker" earliest=-24h
| eval dest=coalesce(DestinationName, destination_name)
| eval qsize=coalesce(QueueSize, queue_size, QueueDepth)
| where match(dest, "(?i)DLQ|dead\\.letter|ActiveMQ\\.DLQ")
| where qsize > 0
| timechart span=15m max(qsize) as dlq_depth by dest, broker_name limit=20
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: this use case is not built on a CIM data model. Rely on the search in Step 2 against your raw logs or metrics.
description: Poison messages and repeated redeliveries land in ActiveMQ dead-letter destinations; any sustained `QueueSize` above zero usually means a consumer or schema defect is starving healthy traffic. Tracking DLQ depth by broker catches regressions after deployments.
value: Surfaces application-level failures that do not trip generic broker health checks but still block business transactions until someone replays or discards bad messages.
grandmaExplanation: We use this to surfaces application-level failures that do not trip generic broker health checks but still block business transactions until someone replays or discards bad messages.
implementation: Normalize DLQ naming (`ActiveMQ.DLQ` vs custom) via lookup. Alert on any non-zero depth for golden paths, or rate-of-change for high-volume environments. Pair with message sampling outside Splunk if bodies contain PII.
visualization: Line chart (DLQ depth), table (broker, destination, depth), single value (total DLQ messages).

references.title: Apache ActiveMQ — Redelivery and Dead Letter

knownFalsePositives: Queues and broker metrics swing during rebalancing, replay, or maintenance. We align with change windows.
